definition: Over 90% of leveraged loans are now covenant-lite, eliminating the traditional credit cycle early warning system. Pre-2010, 25-30% covenant-lite allowed a 12-24 month observable deterioration sequence (covenant violation, amendment, restructuring, default). At >90% saturation, the first two stages are eliminated, compressing observable warning to 3-6 months.
evidence: Traditional 12-24 month deterioration sequence compressed to 3-6 months
Pair_2 debate flagged as standout contribution
Minsky insight: loans skip speculative phase, going from apparent-hedge directly to Ponzi
open_questions: Are there alternative early warning indicators that can substitute for lost covenant-trigger signals?
At what point does the compression become so severe that default clustering creates systemic risk?
